questions.question: The diagram represents the cell structures of a human liver cell, a leaf palisade cell and a human red blood cell. Which cell structure is X?
Which process occurs by diffusion alone?
An experiment to investigate osmosis is set up as shown. What happens to the height of the liquid in tube X and the concentration of the salt solution?
The diagrams show an enzyme molecule and some substrate molecules. Which diagram shows the substrate molecule for this enzyme?
The graphs show factors affecting the rate of photosynthesis. At which points on the graphs could the rate of photosynthesis be limited by light intensity?
The diagram shows the arrangement of cells inside a green leaf. Which types of cells contain chloroplasts?
Why do newly germinated seeds fail to grow into healthy plants if they lack magnesium ions?
Milk contains water, carbohydrates, proteins, fats, and some minerals and vitamins. The graph shows the results of an experiment. What is the explanation for these results?
Which statement about chemical digestion in the human alimentary canal is correct?
The diagram shows a section through a villus. Which products of digestion are mainly absorbed by vessel X?
The diagram shows a section through a plant stem. Which process is responsible for moving substances in region Z?
Which statement describes why a leaf wilts?
How do veins differ from arteries?
The diagram shows a simplified human circulatory system. Which structure represents the heart?
The diagram shows the pressures in the left side of the heart during one heartbeat. Which valves are open and which are closed at the time marked X?
Energy is needed for many processes within the body. Which process does not require the use of energy?
What are features of the alveoli?
Which graph shows a person's breathing when at rest and then exercising?
During kidney dialysis, which blood components pass from the blood into the dialysis fluid?
Which biological process is represented in the diagram?
The diagram shows a front view of the parts of the eye involved in producing a focused image of near and distant objects. What are structures 1 and 2?
The diagram shows some of the nerve pathways associated with a reflex action. If the pathway at X is damaged, how does this affect the reflex?
Which statements about hormones are correct?
The diagram shows the bones of the arm. Which label represents the radius?
An investigation into reaction times involved three similar large groups of adults. What is the best conclusion from the data?
What is a feature of all bacteria?
Yeast is used in alcohol production. What is the other product of this process?
In a pyramid of biomass, the mass of producers is 800 g / m2. What are the likely masses of the carnivores and the herbivores?
Which diagram represents the flow of carbon atoms in an ecosystem?
The diagram shows the nitrogen cycle. Which stages involve bacteria?
Draining stagnant water is one method of controlling the malarial mosquito. Which stages in the mosquito life cycle does this method affect?
Which gas contributes to acid rain?
Which statement is correct about the offspring produced by asexual reproduction?
The graph shows changes in the dry mass of a seed as it germinates and grows. What causes the change shown between points S and T?
The graph shows the concentration in the blood of three of the four hormones FSH, LH, oestrogen and progesterone during a menstrual cycle. Which hormone is not shown?

Infection with HIV can result in death from AIDS. The graph shows AIDS-related deaths from 1990 to 2015 in a country. What is the best explanation for the trend in the number of AIDS-related deaths after 2005?
Some statements about chromosomes are listed. Which statements are correct?
One gene has two codominant alleles, AE and AF, and one recessive allele, AG. How many different genotypes and phenotypes are possible?
What is a result of natural selection?
What is an advantage of genetic engineering?
